prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
(Sonnet Form)

Finishing at last, a sigh of sweet relief,
This long project done, my weary soul can rest.
No more late nights, no more endless grief
Of wondering if my work would transgress

Each part, a battle 'gainst the strict decree
Of time itself, my foe kept gaining ground.
But I persisted, now I'm finally free
To savor the fruits of my labor profound.

And yet, a twinge of sadness too I feel,
For all the hours, days, and months so fast,
Invested in this task, now incomplete,
Another project soon may come to last.

But for now, I'll bask in the afterglow,
Of a monumental feat, my own.
For I have conquered the massive ebb and flow
That comes with any undertone.

So, to myself, I raise my glass to toast,
To the next endeavor, I bid adieu.
